title: Actor-Partner Interdependence Model (APIM)
text: Example model specification:
code: ild_distinguishable_model <- glmmTMB(   closeness ~ 0 +           .i_is_female_x_male_female +      .i_is_female_x_male_male +           # Gender specific time trends     .i_is_female_x_male_female:diaryday +      .i_is_female_x_male_male:diaryday +          # Gender-specific within-person actor effects     .i_is_female_x_male_female:.i_provided_support_cwp_actor +     .i_is_female_x_male_male:.i_provided_support_cwp_actor +      # Gender-specific within-person partner effects     .i_is_female_x_male_female:.i_provided_support_cwp_partner +     .i_is_female_x_male_male:.i_provided_support_cwp_partner +          # Gender-specific between-person actor effects     .i_is_female_x_male_female:.i_provided_support_cbp_actor +     .i_is_female_x_male_male:.i_provided_support_cbp_actor +      # Gender-specific between-person partner effects     .i_is_female_x_male_female:.i_provided_support_cbp_partner +     .i_is_female_x_male_male:.i_provided_support_cbp_partner +          # random effects for stable non-independence (means)     us(0 +           .i_is_female_x_male_female +           .i_is_female_x_male_male         | coupleID)  +      # Same-day residual covariance     us(0 +           .i_is_female_x_male_female +           .i_is_female_x_male_male         | coupleID:diaryday)     , dispformula = ~ 0     , family = gaussian()   , data = ild_distinguishable_data )  summary(ild_distinguishable_model)

what: Interpretation of concurrent ILD DIM coefficients
title: Dyad-Individual Model
text: interpretation applies longitudinally: dyad-mean effects describe expected outcomes individual members expected couple-average outcomes, whereas within-dyad effects describe member deviations expected differences partners. cbp dyad-mean estimate (1.51) means , comparing couples whose average usual support differs one point holding stable difference partners constant, expected couple-average closeness 1.51 points higher higher-support couple. Equivalently, member expected report 1.51 points higher closeness. cwp dyad-mean estimate (0.49) means members one point respective usual support levels, member’s expected closeness 0.49 points higher usual levels, holding difference momentary deviations constant. Equivalently, expected couple-average closeness 0.49 points higher occasion. cbp within-dyad estimate (0.78) means partners differ one point usual support levels, expected differ 0.78 points closeness, holding couple’s average usual support predictors constant. member terms, suppose one member 0.5 points couple’s average usual support 0.5 points . expected closeness 0.39 points couple’s predicted mean, respectively. cwp within-dyad estimate (0.06) means one partner’s momentary deviation usual support one point higher partner’s deviation, expected differ 0.06 points closeness, holding occasion-specific dyad mean predictors constant. member terms, suppose momentary deviations 0.5 points occasion-specific dyad mean. expected closeness 0.03 points couple’s predicted mean, respectively.

what: Equivalence of APIM and DIM in ILD
title: Dyad-Individual Model
text: equivalent APIM uses actor partner effects levels: two ILD models identical fit statistics: equivalence holds separately within-person (cwp) -person (cbp) predictor components. within-person component: βcwp dyad mean=βcwp actor+βcwp partner \beta_{\text{cwp dyad mean}} = \beta_{\text{cwp actor}} + \beta_{\text{cwp partner}} βcwp within-dyad deviation=βcwp actor−βcwp partner \beta_{\text{cwp within-dyad deviation}} = \beta_{\text{cwp actor}} - \beta_{\text{cwp partner}} -person component: βcbp dyad mean=βcbp actor+βcbp partner \beta_{\text{cbp dyad mean}} = \beta_{\text{cbp actor}} + \beta_{\text{cbp partner}} βcbp within-dyad deviation=βcbp actor−βcbp partner \beta_{\text{cbp within-dyad deviation}} = \beta_{\text{cbp actor}} - \beta_{\text{cbp partner}} also means APIM parameterization can used one level DIM parameterization . example: mixed parameterization still estimates model:
code: apim_ILD <- glmmTMB::glmmTMB(   closeness ~     1 +      diaryday +      # Within-person APIM     .i_provided_support_cwp_actor +     .i_provided_support_cwp_partner +      # Between-person APIM     .i_provided_support_cbp_actor +     .i_provided_support_cbp_partner +      # Stable exchangeable dyad-level covariance     (1 | coupleID)  + (0 + .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary | coupleID) +      # Residual (same-day) exchangeable dyad-level covariance     (1 | coupleID:diaryday) +     (0 + .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary | coupleID:diaryday)    , dispformula = ~ 0   , family = gaussian()   , data = ild_exchangeable_data ) data.frame(   model = c("DIM", "APIM"),   AIC = c(AIC(dim_ILD), AIC(apim_ILD)),   BIC = c(BIC(dim_ILD), BIC(apim_ILD)),   logLik = c(as.numeric(logLik(dim_ILD)), as.numeric(logLik(apim_ILD))) ) #>   model      AIC      BIC    logLik #> 1   DIM 2977.225 3026.637 -1478.613 #> 2  APIM 2977.225 3026.637 -1478.613 apim_dim_ILD <- glmmTMB::glmmTMB(   closeness ~     1 +      diaryday +      # Within-person APIM     .i_provided_support_cwp_actor +     .i_provided_support_cwp_partner +      # Between-person DIM     .i_provided_support_cbp_dyad_mean +     .i_provided_support_cbp_within_dyad_dev +      # Stable exchangeable dyad-level covariance     (1 | coupleID)  + (0 + .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary | coupleID) +      # Same-day exchangeable dyad-level covariance     (1 | coupleID:diaryday) +     (0 + .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary | coupleID:diaryday)    , dispformula = ~ 0   , family = gaussian()   , data = ild_exchangeable_data ) data.frame(   model = c("DIM within / DIM between", "APIM within / APIM between", "APIM within / DIM between"),   AIC = c(AIC(dim_ILD), AIC(apim_ILD), AIC(apim_dim_ILD)),   BIC = c(BIC(dim_ILD), BIC(apim_ILD), BIC(apim_dim_ILD)),   logLik = c(     as.numeric(logLik(dim_ILD)),     as.numeric(logLik(apim_ILD)),     as.numeric(logLik(apim_dim_ILD))   ) ) #>                        model      AIC      BIC    logLik #> 1   DIM within / DIM between 2977.225 3026.637 -1478.613 #> 2 APIM within / APIM between 2977.225 3026.637 -1478.613 #> 3  APIM within / DIM between 2977.225 3026.637 -1478.613

title: Dyadic Score Model (DSM)
text: outcomes given predictors, long-format model estimates paths conventional score-based DSM (Iida et al. 2018). Unlike original SEM approach, also model predictor scores provide overall SEM fit test. Define XL=Xfemale+Xmale2−μX,XD=Xfemale−Xmale, X_L = \frac{X_{female} + X_{male}}{2} - \mu_X, \qquad X_D = X_{female} - X_{male}, μX\mu_X sample grand mean dyad-level predictor means. Similarly, define outcome level outcome difference YL=Yfemale+Ymale2,YD=Yfemale−Ymale. Y_L = \frac{Y_{female} + Y_{male}}{2}, \qquad Y_D = Y_{female} - Y_{male}. Although interdep materialize YLY_L YDY_D columns, fitted long-format model estimates two DSM equations: YL̂=a10+a11XL+a12XD, \widehat{Y_L} = a_{10} + a_{11}X_L + a_{12}X_D, YD̂=a20+a21XL+a22XD. \widehat{Y_D} = a_{20} + a_{21}X_L + a_{22}X_D. Observed YLY_L YDY_D scores require partners’ outcomes. long-format model can still use one partner’s available outcome missing; case, equations describe paired expected outcomes rather observed outcome scores dyad. fixed effects map directly paths: Thus, example, a12a_{12} change dyad-average satisfaction associated one-unit larger female-minus-male communication difference, holding communication level constant. contrast, a22a_{22} change female-minus-male satisfaction difference associated one-unit larger communication difference, holding communication level constant. a12a_{12} a21a_{21} coefficients DSM cross-paths. omitted reduced DSM needed full model. random intercept variance unexplained variation outcome level, random role-contrast slope variance unexplained variation full directional outcome difference. covariance indicates whether unexplained outcome level unexplained outcome difference associated.

title: Dyadic Score Model (DSM)
text: Let AfA_f AmA_m denote female male actor effects, let PfP_f PmP_m denote partner effects female male outcomes, respectively. Let αf\alpha_f αm\alpha_m corresponding APIM intercepts. APIM slopes transform female-minus-male DSM paths a11=Af+Pf++Pm2,a12=Af−Pf+Pm−Am4,a21=Af+Pf−−Pm,a22=Af+−Pf−Pm2. \begin{aligned} a_{11} &= \frac{A_f + P_f + A_m + P_m}{2}, & a_{12} &= \frac{A_f - P_f + P_m - A_m}{4}, \\ a_{21} &= A_f + P_f - A_m - P_m, & a_{22} &= \frac{A_f + A_m - P_f - P_m}{2}. \end{aligned} APIM predictors retain original scale, whereas DSM predictor level grand-mean centered. μX\mu_X grand mean subtracted DSM predictor level, intercepts therefore transform a10=αf+αm2+μXa11,a20=αf−αm+μXa21. a_{10} = \frac{\alpha_f + \alpha_m}{2} + \mu_X a_{11}, \qquad a_{20} = \alpha_f - \alpha_m + \mu_X a_{21}. reverse slope transformation Af=a112+a12+a214+a222,Pf=a112−a12+a214−a222,=a112−a12−a214+a222,Pm=a112+a12−a214−a222. \begin{aligned} A_f &= \frac{a_{11}}{2} + a_{12} + \frac{a_{21}}{4} + \frac{a_{22}}{2}, & P_f &= \frac{a_{11}}{2} - a_{12} + \frac{a_{21}}{4} - \frac{a_{22}}{2}, \\ A_m &= \frac{a_{11}}{2} - a_{12} - \frac{a_{21}}{4} + \frac{a_{22}}{2}, & P_m &= \frac{a_{11}}{2} + a_{12} - \frac{a_{21}}{4} - \frac{a_{22}}{2}. \end{aligned} Similarly, αf=a10+a202−μX(a11+a212), \alpha_f = a_{10} + \frac{a_{20}}{2} - \mu_X\left(a_{11} + \frac{a_{21}}{2}\right), αm=a10−a202−μX(a11−a212). \alpha_m = a_{10} - \frac{a_{20}}{2} - \mu_X\left(a_{11} - \frac{a_{21}}{2}\right). following comparison applies APIM--DSM transformation six fixed effects:

title: Getting Started
text: interdep helps researchers prepare cross-sectional intensive longitudinal dyadic data (generalized) multilevel models. automatically creates model-ready columns dyadic multilevel model parameterizations Actor-Partner Interdependence Models (APIM), Dyad-Individual Models (DIM), Dyadic Score Models (DSM). DIM currently supports one exchangeable dyad composition, whereas DSM supports one distinguishable dyad composition. vignette focuses automatic data preparation multilevel models (MLMs). comparison MLM structural equation modeling (SEM) approaches dyadic data, see Ledermann Kenny (2017). guidance examples use prepared data estimate cross-sectional, generalized, intensive longitudinal APIMs, see Actor-Partner Interdependence Model vignette. models combine distinguishable exchangeable dyad compositions, see Mixed-Composition APIM vignette. guidance use Dyad-Individual Model (DIM) parameterization, including dyad-mean within-dyad-deviation predictors equivalence APIM effects (via interactive example) exchangeable dyads, see Dyad-Individual Model vignette. dyadic score parameterization relationship APIM distinguishable dyads, see Dyadic Score Model vignette. -depth tutorial covering data preparation model fitting, also additional steps like diagnostics assumption checks, see Distinguishable Exchangeable Dyads: Bayesian Multilevel Modelling.

title: Getting Started
text: basic data structure needed interdep long data frame dyads stacked top members dyad appear separate rows. raw data currently wide format (time dyads ), reshape long structure using prepare_interdep_data(). See tidyr pivoting vignette pivot_longer() reference. Roughly, expected structure interdep : cross-sectional data: one row per dyad x member intensive longitudinal data: one row per dyad x time x member Measured variables may contain missing values. structural group, member, optional time variables must contain missing values. intensive longitudinal data, missing measurement occasions can represented absent rows, long time variable preserves observed measurement occasions. example: example, row person 1 time 2 absent. time variable preserves observed measurement occasions skips time 1 time 3 person. interdep accepts structure without requiring placeholder row missing occasion.

text: preparation supports contemporaneous models, observations can also remain dependent time. Currently, commonly used open-source MLM interfaces R provide full dyadic residual VAR structure needed model serial dependence across partners. model requires custom TMB Stan code. One practical alternative, especially carryover temporal dynamics part research question, create dynamic model including lagged versions outcome predictors. Lagged versions variables, including outcome also passed predictors, can obtained lag_predictors argument. prepare_interdep_data() returns lag-1 raw within-person-centered actor partner columns alongside contemporaneous versions. Whether raw within-person-centered lagged outcomes used depends research question data. guidance choice, refer Hamaker Grasman (2015) Gistelinck et al. (2021). Brief exchangeable example: simple fixed-slope dyadic stability influence model (del Rosario West 2025): model can extended contemporaneous actor partner predictor associations lagged predictors. Time-varying predictors usually separated within-person -person components. contemporaneous coefficients conditional partners’ prior outcomes. Note: Person-mean centering lagged outcome can introduce Nickell bias, especially shorter time series (Hamaker Grasman 2015; Nickell 1981; Gistelinck et al. 2021). Refer DIM vignette APIM vignette fuller discussions fitted dynamic examples.

mixed_ild_tweedie_model <- glmmTMB(   physical_activity ~ 0 +      # Intercepts for each composition_role     .i_is_female_x_male_female +      .i_is_female_x_male_male +          .i_is_female_x_female +           .i_is_male_x_male +      # C-specific specific time trends     .i_is_female_x_male_female:diaryday +      .i_is_female_x_male_male:diaryday +          .i_is_female_x_female:diaryday +          .i_is_male_x_male:diaryday +      # Composition-specific within-person actor effects     .i_is_female_x_male_female:.i_provided_support_cwp_actor +     .i_is_female_x_male_male:.i_provided_support_cwp_actor +          .i_is_female_x_female:.i_provided_support_cwp_actor +          .i_is_male_x_male:.i_provided_support_cwp_actor +      # Composition-specific within-person partner effects     .i_is_female_x_male_female:.i_provided_support_cwp_partner +     .i_is_female_x_male_male:.i_provided_support_cwp_partner +          .i_is_female_x_female:.i_provided_support_cwp_partner +          .i_is_male_x_male:.i_provided_support_cwp_partner +      # Composition-specific between-person actor effects     .i_is_female_x_male_female:.i_provided_support_cbp_actor +     .i_is_female_x_male_male:.i_provided_support_cbp_actor +          .i_is_female_x_female:.i_provided_support_cbp_actor +          .i_is_male_x_male:.i_provided_support_cbp_actor +      # Composition-specific between-person partner effects     .i_is_female_x_male_female:.i_provided_support_cbp_partner +     .i_is_female_x_male_male:.i_provided_support_cbp_partner +          .i_is_female_x_female:.i_provided_support_cbp_partner +          .i_is_male_x_male:.i_provided_support_cbp_partner +      # stable dyad-level latent covariance     (0 + .i_is_female_x_male_female + .i_is_female_x_male_male | coupleID) +          (0 + .i_is_female_x_female | coupleID) +     (0 + .i_diff_female_x_female_arbitrary | coupleID) +          (0 + .i_is_male_x_male | coupleID) +     (0 + .i_diff_male_x_male_arbitrary | coupleID) +      # same-day latent covariance     (0 + .i_is_female_x_male_female + .i_is_female_x_male_male | coupleID:diaryday) +          (0 + .i_is_female_x_female | coupleID:diaryday) +     (0 + .i_diff_female_x_female_arbitrary | coupleID:diaryday) +          (0 + .i_is_male_x_male | coupleID:diaryday) +     (0 + .i_diff_male_x_male_arbitrary | coupleID:diaryday)      # recommended first simplification in convergence issues is to set dispformula = ~ 1   , dispformula = ~ 0 +       .i_is_female_x_male_female + .i_is_female_x_male_male +       .i_is_female_x_female + .i_is_male_x_male   , family = tweedie()   , data = mixed_ild_tweedie_data ) what: Prepare Dyadic Data for Multilevel Models
title: Prepare Dyadic Data for Multilevel Models
text: interdep helps researchers prepare cross-sectional intensive longitudinal dyadic data multilevel models, including generalized multilevel models. supports dyadic datasets distinguishable dyads, exchangeable dyads, mixed dyad types data, female-male, female-female, male-male couples. creates composition-aware, model-ready columns dyadic multilevel model parameterizations Actor-Partner Interdependence Model (APIM), Dyad-Individual Model (DIM), Dyadic Score Model (DSM). Start vignettes, scroll quick-start. -depth tutorial covering data preparation, model fitting, diagnostics, assumption checks, see Distinguishable Exchangeable Dyads: Bayesian Multilevel Modelling. uses interdep cross-sectional intensive longitudinal APIM DIM workflows, models fitted primarily using brms (source, DOI).

title: Prepare Dyadic Data for Multilevel Models
text: can install development version interdep GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("Pascal-Kueng/interdep")

title: Prepare Dyadic Data for Multilevel Models
text: Prepare distinguishable dyads cross-sectional APIM: prepared data contains composition indicators APIM actor/partner predictor columns used model formulas . One possible APIM formula :
code: library(interdep)  prepared_data <- prepare_interdep_data(   example_dyadic_crosssectional,   group = coupleID,   member = personID,   role = gender,   predictors = communication,   model_type = "apim" )  print(prepared_data, n = 4) #> # interdep data #> # Rows: 190 | Dyads: 95 | Intensive longitudinal: no #> # Structure: group = coupleID, member = personID, role = gender #> # #> # Dyad compositions: #> # female_x_male distinguishable 95 dyads #> # #> # Added columns: #> #   .i_composition       inferred dyad composition #> #   .i_composition_role  composition-specific member role #> #   .i_is_{comp-role}    composition-role indicator columns #> #   .i_{pred}_actor      APIM actor predictor: actor's original predictor #> #                        values #> #   .i_{pred}_partner    APIM partner predictor: partner's original predictor #> #                        values #> # #> # A tibble: 190 × 11 #>   personID coupleID gender communication satisfaction .i_composition #>      <int>    <int> <fct>          <dbl>        <dbl> <fct>          #> 1        1        1 female          4.79         4.37 female_x_male  #> 2        2        1 male            3.80         2.34 female_x_male  #> 3        3        2 female          2.91         2.44 female_x_male  #> 4        4        2 male            6.51         6.08 female_x_male  #> # ℹ 186 more rows #> # ℹ 5 more variables: .i_composition_role <fct>, #> #   .i_is_female_x_male_female <dbl>, .i_is_female_x_male_male <dbl>, #> #   .i_communication_actor <dbl>, .i_communication_partner <dbl> simple_apim <- glmmTMB::glmmTMB(   satisfaction ~       # Gender-specific intercepts     0 + .i_is_female_x_male_female + .i_is_female_x_male_male +      # Gender-specific actor effects     .i_communication_actor:.i_is_female_x_male_female +     .i_communication_actor:.i_is_female_x_male_male +      # Gender-specific partner effects     .i_communication_partner:.i_is_female_x_male_female +     .i_communication_partner:.i_is_female_x_male_male +      # Dyad-level random effects represent the partner residual     # variance-covariance structure     (0 + .i_is_female_x_male_female + .i_is_female_x_male_male | coupleID),    # With the residual variance-covariance represented by the dyad-level   # random effects above, the Gaussian residual dispersion is fixed near zero.   dispformula = ~ 0,   family = gaussian(),   data = prepared_data )

title: Prepare dyadic data for multilevel models — prepare_interdep_data
text: data data frame tibble. Data must long format. cross-sectional dyadic data, observed member dyad one row. intensive longitudinal dyadic data, observed member dyad one row per observed time point. group Column identifying dyad. member Column identifying person member within dyad. role Optional column identifying stable member role, gender. Values must stable within group x member must contain _x_. Missing role information controlled missing_role. role supplied, dyads treated type exchangeable dyads. time Optional column identifying time measurement order repeated measures. predictors Optional variables use temporal predictor decomposition model-ready predictor construction. lag_predictors Optional subset predictors lag-1 model-ready columns created. Requires time finite, integer-valued numeric measurement index. Raw within-person predictor versions lagged; stable -person versions . model_type Model-ready column families construct. Can contain one "apim", "dim", "dsm". "apim" creates actor partner predictors. "dim" creates dyad-mean within-dyad-deviation predictors. "dsm" creates dyadic-score model predictor columns. "none" skips model-specific predictor construction validation, composition inference, optional temporal predictor decomposition, must used alone. "dim" "dsm" must requested separate calls. dsm_role_order model_type = "dsm", character vector giving two distinguishable roles order used directional differences. example, c("female", "male") defines predictor differences female minus male assigns DSM role contrast +0.5 female partners -0.5 male partners. Required DSM columns requested must NULL otherwise. temporal_predictor_decomposition Temporal decomposition strategy predictors. "none" leaves predictors undecomposed model-specific columns constructed. "time_2l" indicates two-level temporal predictor decomposition within-person -person components. "auto" resolves "time_2l" time predictors supplied, "none" otherwise. "time_2l" retains raw model-ready predictors addition within-person -person components. longitudinal DIM DSM construction, raw within-person dyadic scores computed within dyad occasion, -person scores computed within dyads. Raw DIM DSM dyad means grand-mean centered. include raw, within-person, -person versions contemporaneous predictor one model linearly dependent. set_exchangeable_compositions Optionally specify dyad compositions treat exchangeable, roles otherwise imply distinguishability. Requires role. Compositions already exchangeable listed. composition must supplied one string, using _x_, -, _, whitespace ( ) two role labels, example "female_x_male", "female-male", "female_male", "female male", arbitrary order. set multiple compositions, use character vector strings. include_compositions Optional observed dyad compositions keep exchangeability overrides pooling. Requires role. Composition references use format set_exchangeable_compositions. pool_compositions Optionally pool exchangeable dyad compositions shared final composition label. Must named list name final composition label value character vector composition references, example list(same_sex_couples = c("female-female", "male-male")). exchangeable compositions can pooled. pool must contain least two distinct observed compositions composition references resolved. incomplete_dyads handle dyads contain exactly two unique members anywhere data. "error" stops error "drop" removes entire dyad. missing_role handle missing values role column. "error" stops error, "drop" removes dyads incomplete role information. Ignored role column supplied. seed Optional seed random .i_diff_* sign assignment exchangeable dyads. NULL, current R session's RNG state used.

title: Prepare dyadic data for multilevel models — prepare_interdep_data
text: Data must long format. Cross-sectional dyadic data may contain one row per member within dyad. Intensive longitudinal dyadic data may contain one row per member observed measurement occasion within dyad. Measured variables may contain missing values. Missing incomplete structural information controlled incomplete_dyads missing_role. Dyad composition labels canonical: role labels sorted alphabetically combined, labels depend row member order.
